title: Rewrite z_refactor to latest-standard baseline
acceptance: execution artifacts plus the kept blueprint doc are rewritten to latest-standard form and scoped to the current standards plus active backlog

title: Lock current contract boundaries in governance docs
acceptance: the kept governance docs clearly define the split between pipeline frontmatter, UNITS execution, skill assets/references, and helper responsibilities

title: Summarize Anthropic skill design principles for repo use
acceptance: a repo-local note captures Anthropic skill design principles, with special focus on skill-creator and direct implications for this repo

title: Align execution artifacts with the rewritten standard
acceptance: status, checkpoints, decisions, and units all describe the same current workstream and only the active backlog

title: Write the retrieval-informed survey structure design doc
acceptance: a complete design doc defines the target survey workflow as retrieval-informed, section-first, and loop-driven, and the execution artifacts record that design as active input to the next contract migration batch

title: Review the proposed survey structure design and record migration risks
acceptance: a review doc records consensus, minimum contract changes, highest-risk refactor steps, and hidden costs of the proposed survey-structure migration; governance artifacts reflect that review as active input to the next implementation batch

title: Write the survey contract crosswalk and close artifact-lineage ambiguity
acceptance: a crosswalk doc maps current-vs-target survey contract surfaces, explicitly states what is kept/inserted/replaced, and closes ambiguity for mapping.tsv, coverage_report.md, chapter_briefs.jsonl, subsection_briefs.jsonl, and writer_context_packs.jsonl before implementation starts

title: Run a full multi-agent z_refactor re-review and tighten the contract boundary
acceptance: all-agent re-review results are absorbed into the active docs; parse boundary, artifact lineage, checkpoint placement, and backlog sequencing are tightened before implementation begins

title: Introduce metadata-first survey pipeline contract fields
acceptance: survey behavior-critical defaults, variant relation, loop policy, stage contract, and parse boundary live in machine-readable pipeline metadata and are parsed/validated by repo tooling

title: Collapse survey-latex into a survey variant
acceptance: arxiv-survey-latex becomes an explicit variant with only latex/pdf delta instead of a duplicated full contract

title: Extend the metadata-first contract model to idea-brainstorm
acceptance: idea-brainstorm gains machine-readable defaults and validated contract fields under the survey-proven schema instead of relying on prose-only defaults

title: Move survey pipeline defaults out of prose and helper fallbacks
acceptance: survey helpers materialize active contract fields instead of inventing defaults such as core_size, retrieval width, or target counts

title: Move idea-brainstorm defaults out of prose and helper fallbacks
acceptance: idea helpers materialize the active idea contract instead of inventing pool, shortlist, or memo defaults

title: Refactor idea-brief into a reference-first skill package
acceptance: idea-brief owns its reusable method in references, its machine-readable brief contract in assets, and keeps run.py deterministic instead of owning query-bucket policy, rubric rows, and prose templates

title: Align ideation runtime and QA with explicit contract surfaces
acceptance: ideation C4/C5 runtime and QA fail closed on missing pipeline contracts, consume only declared canonical overrides, and stop duplicating idea-brief section requirements outside the asset contract; remaining scoring/selection policy drift is explicitly tracked

title: Insert the section-first survey contract layer into the executable plan
acceptance: survey contracts and units include chapter_skeleton, section_bindings, section_binding_report, section_briefs, stable-H3 cutover state in outline_state, moved structure approval, and explicit reroute surfaces before subsection mapping; chapter-level `hold_or_merge` must materialize as structured `REROUTE` rather than false PASS

title: Align section-logic-polisher acceptance with real runtime enforcement
acceptance: the repo no longer claims `section-logic-polisher` is a hard PASS gate while runtime treats it as diagnostic-only; either the quality gate blocks on FAIL or the unit acceptance/order is revised to match the real semantics

title: Refactor front-matter-writer from prose banks to job/hook contracts
acceptance: front-matter generation stops depending on fixed whole-paragraph banks; Introduction/Related Work are generated from job-level constraints plus candidate hooks, and obvious stock narration such as organization boilerplate / methodology-note self-reference / deictic review talk no longer appear by default

title: Sanitize evidence snippets before writer-context assembly
acceptance: survey-style source narration such as `In this survey`, `In this work`, project-website boilerplate, and other excerpt self-reference are removed or normalized before they reach writer packs, so subsection writers no longer inherit source-paper narration in reader-facing prose

title: Tighten writer-selfloop quality semantics for front matter and sparse H3s
acceptance: front-matter thinness and materially under-cited / underdeveloped H3 bodies block the chain at the right stage instead of surfacing as soft follow-up notes after merge

title: Fix latex-scaffold placeholder leakage and add fail-fast
acceptance: LaTeX scaffold no longer leaks `__LATEX_PH_*__` placeholders into `main.tex`; scaffold or compile QA fails fast if placeholder residue remains after markdown-to-TeX conversion

title: Raise subsection-writer one-shot H3 depth and citation density
acceptance: the clean C5 retest no longer blocks mainly on `sections_h3_sparse_citations` / `sections_h3_too_few_paragraphs`; representative H3 bodies reach the current paragraph-count and local-citation floors without downstream manual patching

title: Remove planner-talk from front matter openings
acceptance: Abstract / Introduction / Related Work no longer rely on planner-talk or contract-talk such as `shared frame`, `opening contract`, `portable contract`, `front-matter wedge`, repeated chapter-organization narration, or axis-list prose; the latest clean retest front matter should read like paper prose rather than planning artifacts

title: De-slot subsection-writer sentence templates and snippet clauses
acceptance: H3 bodies stop leaning on repeated stems such as `The comparison on ...`, `A concrete anchor for the comparison is ...`, `One limitation that changes how the result should be read is ...`; snippet-derived clauses are normalized into natural prose, the remaining weak H3s (`4.1`, `4.2`, `5.1`, `5.3`) recover enough thickness / citation diversity for writer-selfloop, and subsection openings stop falling back to generic slot narration

title: Strengthen front-matter and post-merge voice gates for current smell families
acceptance: the repo now blocks on the planner-talk and slot-phrase families currently visible in the latest PASS draft, instead of letting them pass because they avoid older banned stems

title: De-template chapter-opening glue and keep transitions opt-in
acceptance: H2 lead blocks and adjacent-subsection glue stop serializing outline labels or `Having framed ...` narration into the draft; generated H3 transitions remain opt-in rather than injected by default

title: Recalibrate audit_skills for strict Anthropic-style gating
acceptance: audit_skills distinguishes real leakage/risk from explicit packs, examples, and explanation text and is usable as a strict gate

title: Fix workspace-contract drift in agent-survey-corpus
acceptance: agent-survey-corpus no longer treats repo root as a normal workspace/output location

title: Smoke-validate normalized survey contracts on representative topics
acceptance: representative embodied-AI validation artifacts exist for both survey variants: the historical MD-first pass at `workspaces/arxiv-survey/embodied-ai-smoke-20260311-r1`, the latex variant pass at `workspaces/latex-survey/embodied-ai-smoke-20260311-r2`, and the current clean retest workspace at `workspaces/arxiv-survey/embodied-ai-smoke-20260312-c5-retest` now serves as the live diagnostic surface. Do not sign this unit off until the latest retest itself returns to real PASS (`WRITER_SELFLOOP_TODO.md` PASS, `AUDIT_REPORT.md` PASS, `LATEX_BUILD_REPORT.md` SUCCESS) after the remaining writer-quality cleanup (`U163/U164/U165/U166`) plus helper-default cleanup (`U150`) close
